question: What could be novel candidates as potent inhibitors of papain like cysteine proteases in resistant microorganisms?

docs.text: In summary, the results of the present study have established that 1-substituted pyridylimidazopyridine derivatives could be candidate for novel and potent inhibitors of papain like cysteine proteases, which play deleterious role in the progression of different diseases caused by diverse microorganisms. Therefore, this group of compounds could be the subject of future research to confront the challenges with resistant microorganisms that is a major threat globally.

question: What is the effect of chronic airway inflammatory disease  in patients?

docs.text: Text: The prevalence of chronic airway inflammatory disease is increasing worldwide especially in developed nations . This disease is characterized by airway inflammation leading to complications such as coughing, wheezing and shortness of breath. The disease can manifest in both the upper airway and lower airway which greatly affect the patients' quality of life .

metadata.title: Respiratory Viral Infections in Exacerbation of Chronic Airway Inflammatory Diseases: Novel Mechanisms and Insights From the Upper Airway Epithelium

docs.text: Text: The prevalence of chronic airway inflammatory disease is increasing worldwide especially in developed nations . This disease is characterized by airway inflammation leading to complications such as coughing, wheezing and shortness of breath.

metadata.title: Respiratory Viral Infections in Exacerbation of Chronic Airway Inflammatory Diseases: Novel Mechanisms and Insights From the Upper Airway Epithelium

docs.text: This disease is characterized by airway inflammation leading to complications such as coughing, wheezing and shortness of breath. The disease can manifest in both the upper airway and lower airway which greatly affect the patients' quality of life . Treatment and management vary greatly in efficacy due to the complexity and heterogeneity of the disease.

metadata.title: Respiratory Viral Infections in Exacerbation of Chronic Airway Inflammatory Diseases: Novel Mechanisms and Insights From the Upper Airway Epithelium

docs.text: Furthermore, the destabilization is further compounded by impaired bacterial immune response, either from direct viral influences, or use of corticosteroids to suppress the exacerbation symptoms . All these may gradually lead to more far reaching effect when normal flora is replaced with opportunistic pathogens, altering the inflammatory profiles . These changes may in turn result in more severe and frequent acute exacerbations due to the interplay between virus and pathogenic bacteria in exacerbating chronic airway inflammatory diseases .

metadata.title: Respiratory Viral Infections in Exacerbation of Chronic Airway Inflammatory Diseases: Novel Mechanisms and Insights From the Upper Airway Epithelium

question: What has this focus on mammalian defences and pathologies sidelined?

docs.text: Decades of basic immunology research have provided a near-complete picture of the main armaments in the human antiviral arsenal. Nevertheless, this focus on mammalian defences and pathologies has sidelined examination of the types and roles of viruses and antiviral defences that exist throughout our biosphere. One case in point is the CRISPR/Cas antiviral immune system of prokaryotes, which is now repurposed as a revolutionary gene-editing biotechnology in plants and animals.

question: What was the read coverage for the E-30 genome in this study?

docs.text: average read coverage was 260-fold for the E-30 genome.

metadata.title: Nearly Complete Genome Sequence of an Echovirus 30 Strain from a Cluster of Aseptic Meningitis Cases in California, September 2017

docs.text: The final consensus genome was inspected and annotated using Geneious v10.0.9 . The contig was built from 15,712 reads, assembled to an E-30 reference genome , and deemed nearly complete by comparison to the reference, and the termini were determined as part of the protocol . The total GC content is 48.3% for 7,155 bases.

metadata.title: Nearly Complete Genome Sequence of an Echovirus 30 Strain from a Cluster of Aseptic Meningitis Cases in California, September 2017

docs.text: Data availability. The E-30 sequence of USA/2017/CA-RGDS-1005 has been deposited in GenBank under the accession number MK238483. The quality-filtered FASTQ reads have been deposited in the Sequence Read Archive with the run accession number SRR10082176.

metadata.title: Nearly Complete Genome Sequence of an Echovirus 30 Strain from a Cluster of Aseptic Meningitis Cases in California, September 2017

docs.text: Data availability. The E-30 sequence of USA/2017/CA-RGDS-1005 has been deposited in GenBank under the accession number MK238483.

metadata.title: Nearly Complete Genome Sequence of an Echovirus 30 Strain from a Cluster of Aseptic Meningitis Cases in California, September 2017

docs.text: Population-level characterization requires very high levels of HT sequencing coverage ; however, the exact level will depend on the background error profiles of the sequencing technology and the desired level of sensitivity. As an example, Wang et al. determined that for pyrosequencing data,~400ϫ coverage is necessary to identify minor variants present at 1% frequency with 99.999% confidence, and~1,000ϫ coverage is needed for variants with a frequency of 0.5%.

metadata.title: Standards for Sequencing Viral Genomes in the Era of High-Throughput Sequencing

generation.answer: The read coverage for the E-30 genome in this study was 260-fold, as mentioned in Document 1.

question: How does NLRP3 detect RNA viral infection?

docs.text: Influenza virus-induced NLRP3-mediated IL-1b and IL-18 production involve two steps that include, enhancing the transcription of genes encoding pro-IL-1b and pro-IL-18 and NLRP3 and activating NLRP3 inflammasome . Signal 1 is initiated by the detection of viral RNA by TLR7, which activates NF-kB. Although initial studies showed that NLRP3 detects influenza virus via recognition of viral RNA , it is now clear that many sources contribute to signal 2 either in combination or alone, and all depend on the newly synthesized viral M2 protein.

metadata.title: Innate Immune Sensing and Response to Influenza

docs.text: Thus far, several models have been proposed to explain NLRP3 inflammasome activation by RNA viruses. First, viral RNA or RNA cleavage products generated by RNase L activate the NLRP3 inflammasome via the DExD/H-box helicase, DHX33 . Second, viroporins encoded by RNA viruses activates the NLRP3 inflammasome . In the case of influenza virus, the proton-selective M2 ion channel in the acidic trans-Golgi network activates the NLRP3 inflammasome .

metadata.title: Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus Viroporin 3a Activates the NLRP3 Inflammasome

docs.text: Recognition of virus infection plays an important role in limiting virus replication at the early stages of infection. Nod-like receptor family, pyrin domain-containing 3 is activated by a wide variety of stimuli, including virus infection . Four models describing activation of the NLRP3 inflammasome have been proposed thus far .

generation.answer:
Based on the provided context, there are multiple ways NLRP3 can detect RNA viral infection. 

According to Document 1 and Document 2, initial studies suggested that NLRP3 detects influenza virus via recognition of viral RNA. However, it is now clear that many sources contribute to signal 2, which includes the newly synthesized viral M2 protein.

According to Document 3, there are two models proposed to explain NLRP3 inflammasome activation by RNA viruses. These include:

1. Viral RNA or RNA cleavage products generated by RNase L activating the NLRP3 inflammasome via the DExD/H-box helicase, DHX33.
2. Viroporins encoded by RNA viruses activating the NLRP3 inflammasome.

In the case of influenza virus, the proton-selective M2 ion channel in the acidic trans-Golgi network activates the NLRP3 inflammasome, as mentioned in Document 3.

Therefore, NLRP3 can detect RNA viral infection through the following mechanisms:

- Recognition of viral RNA
- Activation by viral RNA cleavage products generated by RNase L
- Activation by viroporins encoded by RNA viruses
- Activation by the proton-selective M2 ion channel in the acidic trans-Golgi network.

This sentence is supported by sentences that describe the role of the newly synthesized viral M2 protein in NLRP3 activation.
This sentence is not entirely accurate, as Document 2 (not Document 3) describes four models of NLRP3 inflammasome activation.

This sentence is supported by sentences that describe the activation of NLRP3 by the proton-selective M2 ion channel, but the attribution to Document 3 is incorrect (it is actually described in Document 2 and Document 4).

question: How can Bacilius subtilis be used as an oral vaccine?

docs.text: PED virus spreads by fecal–oral contact and can be prevented by oral immunization. Therefore, it is necessary to develop an effective oral vaccine against PEDV infection. Currently, Bacillus subtilis as recombinant vaccine carrier has been used for antigen delivery and proved well in immune effect and safety. The present study evaluated the immunogenicity of recombinant Bacillus subtilis in piglets via oral administration. After oral immunization in piglets, B. subtilis-RC significantly increased the local mucosal immune responses.

metadata.title: Mucosal immune responses induced by oral administration recombinant Bacillus subtilis expressing the COE antigen of PEDV in newborn piglets

docs.text: It is a substantial problem that digestive acids and proteases can degrade antigen proteins for nutrient absorption . Therefore, the vaccine delivery system has been applied to solve the problem. The system can protect antigens from the severe environment of the GI tract and deliver antigens to intestinal mucosa . Currently, Bacillus subtilis is widely used as a vaccine delivery system for its unique characteristics.

metadata.title: Mucosal immune responses induced by oral administration recombinant Bacillus subtilis expressing the COE antigen of PEDV in newborn piglets

docs.text: After oral immunization in piglets, B. subtilis-RC significantly increased the local mucosal immune responses. Oral administration with B.

metadata.title: Mucosal immune responses induced by oral administration recombinant Bacillus subtilis expressing the COE antigen of PEDV in newborn piglets

docs.text: The B. subtilis has adjuvant activity and can deliver heterologous antigens to the GI tract, providing additional immunity stimulation . Besides, research had shown that orally administered B. subtilis could also enhance immune regulation and gut health in pigs . Moreover, oral administration of B. subtilis could elicit humoral and cellular immune responses to the maintenance of gut homeostasis by dendritic cells .

metadata.title: Mucosal immune responses induced by oral administration recombinant Bacillus subtilis expressing the COE antigen of PEDV in newborn piglets

docs.text: Moreover, oral administration of B. subtilis could elicit humoral and cellular immune responses to the maintenance of gut homeostasis by dendritic cells . DCs are the most important professional antigen-presenting cells and can effectively regulate antibody titers .

metadata.title: Mucosal immune responses induced by oral administration recombinant Bacillus subtilis expressing the COE antigen of PEDV in newborn piglets

generation.answer: Bacillus subtilis can be used as an oral vaccine by delivering heterologous antigens to the GI tract, providing additional immunity stimulation. It has adjuvant activity and can protect antigens from the severe environment of the GI tract, allowing them to be delivered to the intestinal mucosa and stimulate a local mucosal immune response. This can elicit both humoral and cellular immune responses, which can help to maintain gut homeostasis and prevent infections such as PEDV.

judge_output.relevance_explanation: The documents discuss the use of Bacillus subtilis as a vaccine carrier and its ability to deliver antigens to the GI tract, making them relevant to the question of how it can be used as an oral vaccine.
judge_output.overall_supported_explanation: While most of the response is supported by the documents, sentence c is only partially supported as the documents do not explicitly mention preventing infections such as PEDV in the context of Bacillus subtilis as an oral vaccine.

sentence_support_information.explanation: This sentence is supported by the documents, which mention that Bacillus subtilis has adjuvant activity and can deliver heterologous antigens to the GI tract.

This sentence is partially supported by the documents, which mention that Bacillus subtilis can protect antigens from the severe environment of the GI tract and deliver them to the intestinal mucosa, but do not explicitly state that it has adjuvant activity in this context.

This sentence is partially supported by the documents, which mention that Bacillus subtilis can elicit humoral and cellular immune responses, but do not explicitly state that this can help maintain gut homeostasis and prevent infections such as PEDV.

question: What is the advantage of AAV vector?

docs.text: AAV vector systems have a number of engaging properties.

metadata.title: Virus-Vectored Influenza Virus Vaccines

docs.text: AAV vector systems have a number of engaging properties. The wild type viruses are non-pathogenic and replication incompetent in humans and the recombinant AAV vector systems are even further attenuated . As members of the parvovirus family, AAVs are small non-enveloped viruses that are stable and amenable to long-term storage without a cold chain.

metadata.title: Virus-Vectored Influenza Virus Vaccines

docs.text: Adeno-associated virus is a parvovirus that is replication-deficient in humans. Like adenovirus, AAV has a broad cell, tissue and host tropism and therefore is a potential good vector vaccine. 218 However, drawbacks of using AAV include: limited capacity for transgenes, presence of pre-existing immunity in humans and the technical challenge of producing high titer stocks.

generation.answer:
Based on the provided context, the advantages of AAV vector systems include:

1. Non-pathogenic and replication incompetent in humans (Document 2).
2. Stable and amenable to long-term storage without a cold chain (Document 2).
3. Broad cell, tissue, and host tropism (Document 3 and Document 4), making it a potential good vector vaccine.
4. Engaging properties (Document 1 and Document 2).

However, the context also mentions some drawbacks of using AAV, such as limited capacity for transgenes, presence of pre-existing immunity in humans, and the technical challenge of producing high titer stocks (Document 3).

judge_output.relevance_explanation: The question asks about the advantages of AAV vector systems. Relevant sentences in the documents discuss the properties and characteristics of AAV vectors, such as being non-pathogenic, stable, and having broad cell tropism.
judge_output.overall_supported_explanation: The response provides some supported advantages of AAV vector systems, but also includes an incorrect attribution of a sentence and a mention of drawbacks without proper context.

sentence_support_information.explanation: The sentence is supported by d1s1, which states that the wild type viruses are non-pathogenic and replication incompetent in humans.
The sentence is supported by d1s2, which states that AAVs are stable and amenable to long-term storage without a cold chain.
The sentence is partially supported by d2s1 and d3s1, which state that AAV has a broad cell, tissue, and host tropism. However, Document 4 is not relevant to this point.
The sentence is supported by d0s0 and d1s0, which state that AAV vector systems have a number of engaging properties.
The sentence is supported by d2s2, which mentions the drawbacks of using AAV. However, the sentence is attributed to Document 3, which does not contain this information.

question: What was the most common virus detected in community members in this sample?

docs.text: Parainfluenza virus 4, respiratory syncytial virus B and enterovirus were the most common viruses in the clinical sample; present in 93.8% of clinical subjects, and 6.7% of community subjects . Coronavirus OC43 was the most common virus detected in community members . A different strain, Coronavirus OC 229 E/NL63 was detected among subjects from the clinic and not detected in the community.

metadata.title: Etiology of respiratory tract infections in the community and clinic in Ilorin, Nigeria

docs.text: Parainfluenza virus 4, respiratory syncytial virus B and enterovirus were the most common viruses in the clinical sample; present in 93.8% of clinical subjects, and 6.7% of community subjects . Coronavirus OC43 was the most common virus detected in community members . A different strain, Coronavirus OC 229 E/NL63 was detected among subjects from the clinic and not detected in the community.

metadata.title: Etiology of respiratory tract infections in the community and clinic in Ilorin, Nigeria

docs.text: These three infections resulted in 41 viruses detected in 15 subjects clinically, and eight infections detected in five people in the community. Together they infected 94% of clinical subjects, and 7% in the community . The most common virus detected in community samples was Coronavirus OC43; this virus was detected in 13.3% people in the community and not in any of the clinical samples.

metadata.title: Etiology of respiratory tract infections in the community and clinic in Ilorin, Nigeria

docs.text: We also compared and contrasted the clinical and community results. Parainfluenza virus 4, respiratory syncytial virus B and enterovirus were the most common viruses found in the clinical sample. These three infections resulted in 41 viruses detected in 15 subjects clinically, and eight infections detected in five people in the community.

This technology allowed, for the first time, the ability to seamlessly connect genetic information with protein function for a large number of protein variants simultaneously, and has been widely and productively exploited in studies of proteinprotein interactions.
